evals.name: links-extraction
evals.prompt: I'm building a sitemap. Give me the full list of links on https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains -- I only care about the link URLs themselves, not the page's article text.
evals.expected_output: A list of links from the page, gathered with browse_links rather than dumping full page text via browse.

assertions.text: Used browse_links (not a full browse text dump) to gather the links
Returned an actual list of link URLs from the page
Did not pull the page's full article body into context

evals.name: targeted-find
evals.prompt: Does the page at https://example.com say anything about needing permission to use the domain? If so, quote the exact sentence.
evals.expected_output: Confirms the page mentions using the domain without prior coordination or asking for permission, quoting the sentence, ideally gathered with browse_find rather than a full page read.

assertions.text: Used browse_find with a query (targeted) rather than reading the whole page
Correctly answers yes and quotes the sentence about permission

evals.name: structure-only
evals.prompt: I want to understand how https://www.iana.org/ is organized. Give me just its heading / section structure. I do not need the body text.
evals.expected_output: Page headings / landmarks gathered with browse_outline rather than a full browse text dump.

assertions.text: Used browse_outline (not full browse text) to get structure
Returned headings / landmarks rather than the full page body
